ref: While Joseph was thinking about this, an angel from the Lord appeared to him in a dream. The angel said, “Joseph, the baby that Mary will have is from the Holy Spirit. Go ahead and marry her.
text: Agi azeegyo eboa la su edi i, Aya yɛ ɔxwɛdzɛɛ ebu isu ya abasɛ Yosef ní kulila mɛ. Lɛ akpɛ ɔna ní yɛ lipo si, “Yosef, Dawid yebie, wukíse lifu ní Maria kugbanɔɔ su u, lɛ ese linu si yɛ limɛ nɛ ɛ, Ɔfuɛfuɛ Dradra lɔ mɛ lidɔ ɔ.
translated: But while he was thinking about these things, an angel of the Lord appeared to him in a dream and said, “Joseph, son of David, do not be afraid to take Mary as your wife, for the child conceived in her is from the Holy Spirit.

ref: So the Lord's promise came true, just as the prophet Jeremiah had said,
text: Niteme liboe lɛ gi Aya yɛ oboedoplɛɛ Yeremya apani aplɛ ɛ,
translated: The word of the Lord came to Jeremiah again, saying,

ref: After King Herod died, an angel from the Lord appeared in a dream to Joseph while he was still in Egypt.

translated: When Herod died, an angel of the Lord appeared in a dream to Joseph in Egypt,

ref: But when he heard that Herod's son Archelaus was now ruler of Judea, he was afraid to go there. Then in a dream he was told to go to Galilee,
text: Pɔ̀ gi Yosef enu si Arxelao yɛ ɛɛŋa kukusi yo ní yɛka Herodes kutse o kede ní Yudea ɛ, ese lifu ne ki niklɔ kutrɛtrɛ. Agi bɛkpɛ yɛ ɔsã ní kulilaa mɛ loso e, ekyi atrɛ ní Galilea kesea mɛ.
translated: But when Joseph heard that Archelaus was reigning in Judea in place of his father Herod, he was afraid to go there. Having been warned in a dream, he withdrew to the district of Galilee.

ref: Do something to show you have really given up your sins.
text: Mlabitɛ eboa la gi aabasɛ si mlekyi ní mlɔ ina nɛ mɛ ɛ.
translated: The children went to the farm yesterday but they did not see anything.

ref: Then a voice from heaven said, “This is my own dear Son, and I am pleased with him.”

ref: But instead of staying in Nazareth, Jesus moved to Capernaum. This town was beside Lake Galilee in the territory of Zebulun and Naphtali.

translated: And leaving Nazareth, he went and lived in Capernaum, which is by the lake in the area of Zebulun and Naphtali.

ref: You are the light for the whole world. A city built on top of a hill cannot be hidden,
text: “Mlalɛ ŋwisi kani aki kelea mɛ. Ɔma gi bɛtsa ní lito aʋa ɛ, óʋikpo o.

translated: If a person goes to the farm and returns and is tired, he eats and rests, and when he is strong he goes back to the farm. If it is not so, he will be tired, and will not be able to do the work well, or will not be able to finish the work he started. That is how it is with life.

ref: I promise you will not get out until you have paid the last cent you owe. Marriage
text: Sugba sugba ɛ, níklɔ wɔaze kɔkpɛni lipoe lɛ gi wɔatsa liʋɛ kakaa gi bɛnya ɛki wɔ ɛ. Yesu abasi bidɔ ní ádzɛboe nì enyimeboe su u
translated: The elders say, if you want to build a strong house, you must first lay a good foundation. Jesus is the foundation and the cornerstone of our faith.

ref: You have heard people say, “Love your neighbors and hate your enemies.”

ref: But you must always act like your Father in heaven.

ref: When you do good deeds, don't try to show off. If you do, you won't get a reward from your Father in heaven.
text: “Mledi lɛ aʋa si, mlakíbitɛ mlɔ eboe gbã na ní banima aŋu mɛ tɔ bamɔ ɔ. Pɔ̀ xe mlɛbitɛ nite, Ɔkaɛ ní lifunu ne ɔ́tratsa liʋɛ tɔtɔ ɛki mlɔ ɔ.
translated: When the day comes, everyone will gather together and talk about what happened. If they agree, the elders and the youth will eat and drink together.

ref: When you pray, don't talk on and on as people do who don't know God. They think God likes to hear long prayers.
text: “Xe wɔɛxwa Aya ɛ, wukízeege eboe la epio ŋwisi kiliɛ gi babubitɛ ba bizɛbitɛ kinɛ ɛ, lɛ ese linu si bebu si xegi ba eboe la ɛsrɛ ɛ, kɔ ba Aya enu aki ba ɛ.
translated: When Aya was young, her grandmother would tell her stories about the old days, and she loved to listen to them and remember them, so Aya grew up with them.

ref: If you forgive others for the wrongs they do to you, your Father in heaven will forgive you.
text: Lɛ “Xe mlɛkɔ banima eboe zimizi na ní mlɔ su i, kyɛ ba ɛ, mlɔ Ɔka ní lifunu ne fɛ aakɔ̀ mlɔ ani na kyɛ mlɔ.
translated: “If you do not forgive people their sins, your Father will not forgive your sins.”

text: Pɔ xe mlákɔ banima eboe zimizi na akyɛ ba ɛ, mlɔ Ɔka fɛ ɔ́trakɔ mlɔ eboe zimizi na ɛkyɛ mlɔ. Yesu abasi bidɔ ní onugu nyanya su
translated: If you do good things for others, God will also do good things for you. Jesus loves everyone and wants us to love each other.

text: Mledi liwɔ lɛ mɛ kudzɔʋi yɔ; kuʋíkpɛ bidɔ loo vini bidɔ kɔbla ní owle me, pɔ fɛ ɛ mlɔ Ɔka yɛ ní lifunu ne e izeedi kɔ aʋa. Mláŋa kedzi asani kɔ na a?
translated: The child is not here today; the children who used to play here have gone to the farm, so I saw Oka with his friend as they were coming. Should I wait here or go?

ref: Everyone who asks will receive. Everyone who searches will find. And the door will be opened for everyone who knocks.

translated: If you do this, you will get some things, if you do that, you will get other things, but if you do nothing, you will get nothing at all.

translated: The chief said; If someone greets you, and you do not respond to the greeting, then you have not respected the greeting, and you have not shown respect to the person, so do not expect good things in the community.

translated: The children went to the house, and they greeted their mother. Then they sat down and ate food, and they thanked their mother. After that, they went outside to play.

translated: If someone wants to do something good, let him do it; but if he does not, let him be, because everyone has their own way of thinking.

translated: He/she is not here. That is why, when you came, you did not see the person you were looking for.

translated: But when Joseph heard that Archelaus was reigning in Judea in place of his father Herod, he was afraid to go there. Having been warned in a dream, he withdrew to the district of Galilee.

text: Mlabitɛ eboa la gi aabasɛ si mlekyi ní mlɔ ina nɛ mɛ ɛ.
translated: The child is playing with the ball in front of the house.

ref: Then a voice from heaven said, “This is my own dear Son, and I am pleased with him.”
text: Lɛ ole ɔtɔ ɛpani dɔni lifunu ne si, “Mobi ye liɛ ya yɛ madimɛ nɔ yɛ liboe liku me aŋu u!”
translated: "Then the man called his wife and said, 'Come here and let us go and see what is happening!'"

ref: But instead of staying in Nazareth, Jesus moved to Capernaum. This town was beside Lake Galilee in the territory of Zebulun and Naphtali.
text: Asɛ ní Nazaret lɛ adzɛ eze ní Kapernaum, gi onu ɔma ɔtɔ ní Galilea limu ne kutu ní Zebulon ni Naftali kuseo me e.
translated: And leaving Nazareth, he went and lived in Capernaum, which is by the lake in the area of Zebulun and Naphtali.

ref: Then the devil came to him and said, “If you are God's Son, tell these stones to turn into bread.”
text: Lɛ Ozimizi ye aba yɛ klɔ ɔ; lɛ asi yɛ si, “Xe Aya Yebi wonu i, kɔlɔ ki tɔ akla la ya edzì bidɔŋaŋa aki wɔ.”
translated: And Ozimizi came and stood there; and he said, “Tell Aya Yebi to come, for there is something important I want to discuss with him.”

ref: Jesus walked on until he saw James and John, the sons of Zebedee. They were in a boat with their father, mending their nets. Jesus asked them to come with him.
text: Yesu atrɛ ní ɔtɔ nɔ lɛ azɛɛmɔ beneminyime tiaʋa dzedze e. Ba beze Yakobo ni Yohanes, gi benu Zebedeo yɛ bebi ba. Beze ní ba katapokpa me ni ba ɔkaɛ gi bɛzɛlulɔ ba dzikle ye e. Niteme si Yesu axwa ba kyɛ.
translated: Jesus walked along the shore and saw two brothers fishing. They were James and John, the sons of Zebedee. They were in a boat with their father, preparing their nets. Jesus called them.

ref: and he taught them:
text: Lɛ ekpese ba bidɔ kubasɛ si,
translated: And the child brought a cup here,

ref: God blesses those people who grieve. They will find comfort!
text: Isami iku banama gi bɛwa awowoe la ɛ, lɛ ese linu si Aya aayɔ ba ina a.
translated: Isami said that they should not go there, but Aya insisted on going.

ref: You are the light for the whole world. A city built on top of a hill cannot be hidden,
text: “Mlalɛ ŋwisi kani aki kelea mɛ. Ɔma gi bɛtsa ní lito aʋa ɛ, óʋikpo o.
translated: The child is not here today. If you see him/her, tell him/her to come home.

ref: The earth is God's footstool, so don't swear by the earth. Jerusalem is the city of the great king, so don't swear by it.
text: Mlakítani kesea, lɛ ese linu si ka kenu Aya yɛ ikli lɛ odu klɔ ɔ. Mlakítani Yerusalem kyɛ ɛ lɛ ese linu si onu Okusi ʋidi ye yɛ ɔma.
translated: The disciples asked, "Lord, is this the time when you will restore the kingdom to Israel?" The disciples were in Jerusalem because the Lord told them to wait for the promise.

ref: Before you are dragged into court, make friends with the person who has accused you of doing wrong. If you don't, you will be handed over to the judge and then to the officer who will put you in jail.
text: “Xe ɔnutɔ akɔ liboe ní wɔ su ní otsixwakɔ ɔ, xe lipoe le lilɛ ɛ, ye liboe le nì yɛ xe litsixwawe le liana a. Xegi aní nite e, xe mleku otsixwaklɔ ɛ, aakɔ̀ wɔ kukpɛ awla ki otsixwa yɛ, kɔ yɛ kyɛ akɔ wɔ akpɛ awla aki bewukpɛ ʋa ɛ. Niteme si biakpɛ wɔ ligba.
translated: If a person wants to farm and there is land, and the farm is good, then the person will farm and get food. But if it is not so, if the land is not good, then he will go to another place to farm, or he will look for another work to do. That is how life is.

ref: I promise you will not get out until you have paid the last cent you owe. Marriage
text: Sugba sugba ɛ, níklɔ wɔaze kɔkpɛni lipoe lɛ gi wɔatsa liʋɛ kakaa gi bɛnya ɛki wɔ ɛ. Yesu abasi bidɔ ní ádzɛboe nì enyimeboe su u
translated: After that, the people brought children to him so that he would touch them. But the disciples rebuked those who brought them.

ref: You have heard people say, “Love your neighbors and hate your enemies.”
text: “Mleenu gi bɛpani si, ‘Mlalɔ mlɔ ahanuglɔ ba atɔ mlekyri mlɔ ahwafianɛ ma.’
translated: "The child said, 'I will go to the farm and bring back firewood for you.'"

ref: If you greet only your friends, what's so great about this? Don't even unbelievers do that?
text: Lɛ xegi mlɔ baʋia nɛ ba ko mlezeegu kunugu yo nɔ e, kiboebitɛ gbã woli lu mlɛsa kubitɛ ɛ? Banama gi béte Aya ɛ, ani nite ko ba kyɛ bizɛbitɛ ɛ?
translated: If someone gives you something and says it is a gift, should you still ask where they got it from? Or should you just accept it, especially if you don't know the person well?

ref: But you must always act like your Father in heaven.
text: Mlaki si mlɔ kizezee me kiflɔ ŋwisi kiliɛ gi mlɔ Ɔka yɛ ní lifunu ne aflɔ kinɛ ɛ.
translated: The woman went to the market and bought fish because she wanted to cook food for her children.

ref: When you do good deeds, don't try to show off. If you do, you won't get a reward from your Father in heaven.
text: “Mledi lɛ aʋa si, mlakíbitɛ mlɔ eboe gbã na ní banima aŋu mɛ tɔ bamɔ ɔ. Pɔ̀ xe mlɛbitɛ nite, Ɔkaɛ ní lifunu ne ɔ́tratsa liʋɛ tɔtɔ ɛki mlɔ ɔ.
translated: The child who came here, his mother said that he should not go outside at night. If he goes outside, the spirit called Lifunu will catch him and take him away.

ref: When you pray, don't talk on and on as people do who don't know God. They think God likes to hear long prayers.
text: “Xe wɔɛxwa Aya ɛ, wukízeege eboe la epio ŋwisi kiliɛ gi babubitɛ ba bizɛbitɛ kinɛ ɛ, lɛ ese linu si bebu si xegi ba eboe la ɛsrɛ ɛ, kɔ ba Aya enu aki ba ɛ.
translated: When Aya was young, she used to go to the farm with her siblings and help their mother with the work, but now that she is grown, she goes to the farm alone.

ref: If you forgive others for the wrongs they do to you, your Father in heaven will forgive you.
text: Lɛ “Xe mlɛkɔ banima eboe zimizi na ní mlɔ su i, kyɛ ba ɛ, mlɔ Ɔka ní lifunu ne fɛ aakɔ̀ mlɔ ani na kyɛ mlɔ.
translated: “If someone slaps you on one cheek, do not retaliate, but rather, turn the other cheek to them as well.”

ref: But if you don't forgive others, your Father will not forgive your sins. Worshiping God by Going without Eating
text: Pɔ xe mlákɔ banima eboe zimizi na akyɛ ba ɛ, mlɔ Ɔka fɛ ɔ́trakɔ mlɔ eboe zimizi na ɛkyɛ mlɔ. Yesu abasi bidɔ ní onugu nyanya su
translated: If you have something good in your hand and you give it to someone, you will also receive something good in return. Jesus said we should do to others what we want them to do to us.

ref: Don't worry and ask yourselves, “Will we have anything to eat? Will we have anything to drink? Will we have any clothes to wear?”
text: “Lɛ loso e, mlakítà ɔna ni mlɔ begyodi ʋa si, ege kwíatà, ege kwiaŋa, ege kwiaŋwɛ loo ege kwiakpɛ ɛ?
translated: And then, if I take this and go to the farm, will I find plantains, yams, cocoyams or cassava?

ref: Look at the birds in the sky! They don't plant or harvest. They don't even store grain in barns. Yet your Father in heaven takes care of them. Aren't you worth much more than birds?
text: Mledi liwɔ lɛ mɛ kudzɔʋi yɔ; kuʋíkpɛ bidɔ loo vini bidɔ kɔbla ní owle me, pɔ fɛ ɛ mlɔ Ɔka yɛ ní lifunu ne e izeedi kɔ aʋa. Mláŋa kedzi asani kɔ na a?
translated: The child is not here today; the children who usually play here have gone to the farm, so I am waiting for Akah to bring the food before I come. Should I wait outside or what?

ref: God gives such beauty to everything that grows in the fields, even though it is here today and thrown into a fire tomorrow. God will surely do even more for you! Why do you have such little faith?
text: Xegi nite Aya izɛlulɔ siwa sɛ ɛ, gi silɛ omono o, xe kivo biaŋwia sɛ ɔkpɛ kifu mɛ ɛ, ɔ́tratani mlɔ kululɔ ni bidɔkpɛkpɛ ʋɛ sanɔ na a? Mlɔ kuziŋaa kuli sisami koŋ!
translated: When Aya was asked about it, she said she did not know, but she thought maybe it was because the children did not listen to their parents. What do you think about this matter? I think it is very important!

ref: Everyone who asks will receive. Everyone who searches will find. And the door will be opened for everyone who knocks.
text: Lɛ ese linu si, ɔnɔ kakaa gi eeʋi bidɔ ɛ, bizɛki yɛ, lɛ ɔnɔ kakaa gi ɛɛpɛ bidɔ ɛ, aamɔ ʋɛ ɛ, ɔnɔ kakaa gi aasa opupo lo e, biadra lɔ ɔki yɛ.
translated: If you do good to a child, he will remember it; if you do bad to a child, he will also remember it, and if you do something to an adult, he will not forget it.

ref: On the day of judgment many will call me their Lord. They will say, “We preached in your name, and in your name we forced out demons and worked many miracles.”
text: Ní litsixwawe le aʋa ɛ, banɔ glegle biaŋwlɛ isu ya litukpo si, ‘Ɔka, Ɔka! Wɔ línyi ne me kwipani wɔ Liboe le e, lɛ wɔ linyi ne me kwikpikpini ifuɛfuɛ zimizi glegle e dono o, lɛ wɔ linyi ne me kwibitɛ enugutseboe glegle la!’
translated: And the birds of the sky said, ‘Look, Look! If you make me into a human being, I will not become a person who eats food and forgets others, I will not become a person who takes all the good things for himself, I will not become a person who hoards riches for himself!’

ref: But I will tell them, “I will have nothing to do with you! Get out of my sight, you evil people!” Two Builders (Luke
text: Nite si maasi ba wee si, “Méte mlo liwewe e, Mlasɛ ní mɛ su, mlɔ banɔ dovo ba!” Bagbawa tiaʋa ba a
translated: "At night the woman said, 'I am very tired, tomorrow I will go, I will come back in the afternoon!' The children agreed with her."

ref: But the ones who should have been in the kingdom will be thrown out into the dark. They will cry and grit their teeth in pain.”
text: Pɔ̀ banama gi ba kaŋase kenu Kaʋaŋaa xe e, Aya aaŋwia ba ɛkpɛ ní litumu ne me. Niklɔ biabemi xe biatà ba ene na.”
translated: When they arrived at the house of Kavana, Aya’s mother greeted them and invited them to sit down. She asked them why they had come.

ref: Everyone in town came out to meet Jesus. When they saw him, they begged him to leave their part of the country.
text: Nite si banima pɔ́ gi bɛdɔ ɔma nɔ mɛ ɛ, bedo gi bɛzɛtsani Yesu. Agi bɛmɔ yɛ ɛ, bɛ bɛki yɛ litukpo si ɔsɛ ní ba kesea mɛ.
translated: That night some people came to arrest him, they took him to Jesus. When they arrived, they stood at the place where he was being judged.

ref: So God's promise came true, just as the prophet Isaiah had said, “He healed our diseases and made us well.” Some Who Wanted To Go with Jesus (Luke
text: Abitɛ lɛ nite tɔ yiki liboe lɛ gi Aya yɛ oboedoplɛ Yesaya apani i liba lɛ mɛ si, “Akɔ blɔ kubotsobotsoo atrɔ isu ya aʋa lɛ etu blɔ bànya ʋa.” Banɔ tiaʋa bɛpɛ si biakuni Yesu kude
translated: This happened to fulfill what was spoken by the prophet Isaiah, who said, “A voice is calling out in the wilderness, ‘Prepare the way of the Lord.’” After this, Jesus began his work.

ref: Another disciple said to Jesus, “Lord, let me wait till I bury my father.”
text: Yesu yɛ ɔdɔkpasi dzedze apani si, “Ɔka, kpɛ ɔtɔ ɛ, kí tɔ midzitrɛ tɔ mizeevive mɔka.”
translated: Jesus is the good shepherd who says, “Look, here I am, I have come so that you may have life abundantly.”

ref: After Jesus left in a boat with his disciples,
text: Yesu emu eku ní katapokpaa mɛ lɛ yɛ badɔkpasi ba bekuni yɛ kude e.
translated: Jesus himself will come again to judge the living and the dead.

ref: Not far from there a large herd of pigs was feeding.
text: Iha kisrɛsrɛ ɛtɔ iizɛŋa bidɔ mɛ ní niklɔ ɔza lɔ.
translated: The children are playing under the big tree.

ref: So the demons begged Jesus, “If you force us out, please send us into those pigs!”
text: Nite si ifuɛfuɛ zimizi ne iiki Yesu litukpo si, “Xe wɔtra kpikpini blɔ edono e, kɔ lɔɔ ki tɔ kutiku ní iha lɛ likpɔkpɛkpɛ lɛ klɔ mɛ.”
translated: One day while Jesus was teaching, he said, “If you have faith as small as a mustard seed, you can say to this mountain, ‘Move from here to there,’ and it will move.”

ref: But Jesus knew what was in their minds, and he said, “Why are you thinking such evil things?
text: Agi ba begyodi ʋa bezee kutete me ɛki Yesu loso e, eʋi ba si, “Ege lɛ loso mleegyo eboe zimizi na edi ní mlɔ ina nɛ mɛ ɛ?
translated: "When they brought the children to Jesus, he said, 'Let the children come to me and do not stop them, for the kingdom of God belongs to such as these.'"

ref: After Jesus had gone indoors, the two blind men came up to him. He asked them, “Do you believe I can make you well?” “Yes, Lord,” they answered.
text: Agi Yesu eti eku ní kepea mɛ ɛ, benyime tiaʋa ba klɔ gi ba bimbi ʋe bini i bɛba yɛ klɔ, lɛ eʋi ba si, “Mlɛzi lɛ aʋa ɛɛŋa si maatani mlɔ bimbi ʋe kudra na?” Bebu le onu si, “Ee, Ɔkaɛ.”
translated: When Jesus had crossed over again by boat to the other side, a large crowd gathered around him while he was by the lake. Then one of the synagogue leaders, named Jairus, came, and when he saw Jesus, he fell at his feet. He pleaded earnestly with him, "My little daughter is dying. Please come and put your hands on her so that she will be healed and live." So Jesus went with him.

ref: He said to his disciples, “A large crop is in the fields, but there are only a few workers.
text: Lɛ asi yɛ badɔkpasi ba si, ɔnyrɔ nɔ mɛ bidɔ mɛ bisrɛ, pɔ̀ baxwɛwa gi biakpɛ ʋɛ likpɔ básrɛ ɛ.
translated: If you want to do something good, you should ask for advice from those who have done it before.

ref: Brothers and sisters will betray one another and have each other put to death. Parents will betray their own children, and children will turn against their parents and have them killed.
text: Beneminyime biakpɛ ba beneminyime ní awla tɔ beye; lɛ bebika batɔ kyɛ biabitɛ nite ke tsini ba bebi ba su u, bebi biayɔ kedu ní ba bapɔnɛ ba su gi biaki biaye ba a.
translated: The elders say that if the elders do not sit and talk, the children will not know what to do; so they say that when the elders sit and talk, the children listen and learn what they should do.

ref: Because of me, you will be dragged before rulers and kings to tell them and the Gentiles about your faith.
text: Kɔ kpɛ mɛ loso e, biaxwa litsi ni mlo ní baʋaŋa ba ani bekusi ʋidi ba aŋu mɛ; lɛ gani lɛ mɛ ɛ, mlaapani liboe Gbã nɛ kí bekusi ba ni banama gi bénu Yadanɛ ma kyɛ ɛ.
translated: Go and tell him, the children should come and greet me before they eat; if not, I will report them to God so that he will not allow them to enter Heaven.

ref: But you will not really be the one speaking. The Spirit from your Father will tell you what to say.
text: Lɛ ese linu si mlátrapani ní mlɔ isuwla mɛ ɛ. Pɔ̀ eboe la aadɔ ní mlɔ Ɔka yɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ klɔ gi ɛɛpani gani mlɔ aʋa ɛ.
translated: They said that the children should not go outside today. But if it rains and the sky becomes clear, then they can go out and play.

ref: Students are not better than their teacher, and slaves are not better than their master.
text: Ɔdɔkpasibi ɔtɔtɔ óʋikeme ɔsani yɛ ɔdɔbasɛ yɛ; lɛ ɛ ɔnɔdzidzi ɔtɔtɔ óʋimu ɔsani yɛ ɔkaɛ ɛ.
translated: True love is like a precious jewel; when you find it, cherish it always.

ref: Aren't two sparrows sold for only a penny? But your Father knows when any one of them falls to the ground.
text: Tiasɛ kura abia taʋa izɛɛzɛ ɛ? Pɔ̀ libia ɛtɔtɔ litrawɔli dɔ kesea xegi mlɔ ɔka Aya ɔ́ki kudeonu u.
translated: What is the child doing there? He is sitting under the big tree and watching Aya as she sweeps the compound.

ref: Even the hairs on your head are counted.
text: Mlɔ kɔ ɛ, mlɔ litukpo le siya sɛ kyɛ ɛ, Aya ete sɛ kiklaklaɛ mɛ ɛ.
translated: If I go, I will return, but if I do not return, Aya will cry for me.

ref: So don't be afraid! You are worth much more than many sparrows. Telling Others about Christ (Luke
text: Lɛ loso e, mlakíse lifu u. Lɛ ese linu si, mlɛŋa kedzi isani abia likpi. Yesu yɛ su kudradra ni yɛ kugbegbe
translated: So then, let us be careful. If we do this, we will have good things in our lives. Jesus is the light and He is the way.

ref: Don't think I came to bring peace to the earth! I came to bring trouble, not peace.
text: “Mlakígyo le edi si kusuyɔ mamanɔ ní kelea mɛ ɛ. Owo, mámani kusuyɔ ɔ, pɔ̀ kusi lɛlɛ kwitraze e.
translated: The child is at home with his mother today. No, his mother is not at home, she went to the market.

ref: If you try to save your life, you will lose it. But if you give it up for me, you will surely find it. Rewards (Mark
text: Ɔniliɛ gi aapɛ si yibu yɛ ligbã nɛ ɛ, aalila lɛ. Pɔ̀ ɔniliɛ gi aalila yɛ ligbã nɛ kɔkpɛ mɛ loso e, aabu le. Liʋɛtsatsa ní Yesu yɛ badɔkpasi kuzɛɛzɛ loso
translated: The woman who was sick for a long time was healed. When the woman touched the edge of his garment, she was healed. Faith in Jesus brings complete healing.

ref: Anyone who welcomes a prophet, just because that person is a prophet, will be given the same reward as a prophet. Anyone who welcomes a good person, just because that person is good, will be given the same reward as a good person.
text: Ɔnɔ kakaa gi azɛ Aya yɛ oboedoplɛ ɛ, ní kiliɛ gi onu Aya yɛ oboedoplɛ onu i, aamɔ liʋɛtsatsa ŋwisi oboedoplɛ kinɛ ɛ. Lɛ ɔniliɛ klɔ gi aazi ɔnɔ gi ɔlɛ ní kude gyɔgyɔ aʋa kɔ kpɛ gi ɔlɛ ní kude gyɔgyɔ aʋa loso e, aamɔ liʋɛtsatsa ŋwisi ɔnɔ gi yɛ linɛ ligyɔ kinɛ ɛ.
translated: If someone says that Aya is a good person, and another says that Aya is not a good person, then there is confusion about who is good. But if everyone says the same thing about someone, whether they say good things or bad things about them, then there is no confusion about who they are.

ref: John the Baptist did not go around eating and drinking, and you said, “That man has a demon in him!”
text: Agi Yohanes aba ɛ azaanya onugu lo lɛ ɔ́ŋwɛ wain kyɛ ɛ; lɛ ɔnɔ kakaa apani si, ‘Ɔfuɛfuɛ zimizi ɔkpasi ní yɛ mɛ!’
translated: John saw Jesus coming toward him and said, ‘Look, the Lamb of God who takes away the sin of the world!’

ref: “We played the flute, but you would not dance! We sang a funeral song, but you would not mourn!”
text: “ ‘kwikpɛ kudzɛgbanɔ idzi iki mlɔ, pɔ̀ mleyo o. Kwikpɛ ibemidzi i ki mlɔ, pɔ̀ mlébemi i.’
translated: "‘A clever person does not say all that they know, but keeps quiet. A fool says all that they know, but keeps nothing.’"

ref: In the towns where Jesus had worked most of his miracles, the people refused to turn to God. So Jesus was upset with them and said:
text: Agi banama gi bali ní imaɛ lɛ klɔ mɛ gi Yesu abitɛ yɛ enugutseboe la begbe xe békyi ní ba eboe zimizi na mɛ ɛ, Yesu atɛni ba a.
translated: When they arrived there, they saw Jesus sitting with his disciples and he was teaching them, Jesus himself was there.

ref: My Father has given me everything, and he is the only one who knows the Son. The only one who truly knows the Father is the Son. But the Son wants to tell others about the Father, so they can know him too.
text: “Mɛ ɔka akɔ kidɔ kakaa aki mɛ. Ɔnɔnɔ óte Obi ye sani Ɔka yɛ ko, lɛ ɔnɔnɔ fɛ óte Ɔkaɛ, sani Obi ye ko, ani banama gi Obi ye apɛ si yibu ye e basɛ ɛ.
translated: If I go to the farm today. Whether I meet Obi or not, if I meet Kae, and Obi is not there, then I will tell Obi what happened when I see him.

ref: but warned them not to tell anyone about him.
text: Lɛ etsi kude akí ba si, bakípani ní yɛ su asi ɔnɔnɔ ɔ.
translated: If you don't want to go, tell me so that I know.

ref: So God's promise came true, just as Isaiah the prophet had said,
text: Abitɛ lɛ nite atɔ yikɔ ebu í basɛ si, liboe lɛ gi Aya apani agani yɛ oboedoplɛ Yesaya aʋa ɛ liba lɛ mɛ si:
translated: The people who walked in darkness have seen a great light, those who lived in a land of deep darkness—on them light has shined.

ref: “Here is my chosen servant! I love him, and he pleases me. I will give him my Spirit, and he will bring justice to the nations.
text: “Mɛ ɔwxɛdzɛɛ gi makya ɛ, yɛ iliɛ ya, yɛ onu ɔniliɛ gi madimɛ nɔ xe mamɔ isami ní yɛ su u. Maakpɛ mɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ ní yɛ mɛ; aasa kamasa ní mɛ litsi me kutsatsa dradra su u ki banima petee ní kelea mɛ.
translated: If you hear my voice today, do not harden your heart, do not be like those who rebelled against me in the wilderness. I am the Lord your God; walk in my ways and I will give you peace and bless you with good things.

ref: Some people brought to Jesus a man who was blind and could not talk because he had a demon in him. Jesus healed the man, and then he was able to talk and see.
text: Liba lɛ mɛ si banɔatɔ bɛgbani onyime ɔtɔ mɛ gi ifuɛfuɛ zimizi ize e manɔ ní Yesu klɔ. Niteme onyimee, eze kaŋupo ni omúmu. Yesu ayɔni yɛ, niteme si ekpese kunugu yo kugu lɛ azɛɛtani kumɔ kyɛ ɛ.
translated: God has given us eternal life and this life is in his Son. Whoever has the Son has life; whoever does not have the Son of God does not have life.

ref: Jesus knew what they were thinking, so he said to them: Any kingdom where people fight each other will end up ruined. And a town or family that fights will soon destroy itself.
text: Yesu ete ba begyodi ʋa, lɛ asi ba si,” Kaʋaŋaa kakaa gi kalɛ ɛ, xe kɛyɔ kedu ya ní ka isu ya su i, kiayaʋi. Lɛ ɔma loo kepe kakaa mɛ gi kulɛlɛ nì eboese monumonu bi lɛ ɛ, kiakyrɔ̃.
translated: Jesus said to them, "Give them something to eat." They replied, "We have only five loaves of bread and two fish, unless we go and buy food for all this crowd." There were about five thousand men there.

text: Ɔʋla lɔ gi eyi lo o lɔ onu si: ‘Lɔ wɔ ɔhanuglɔɛ ŋwisi wɔ ɔtutɔ wɔ isu ya.’
translated: The chief said to them: ‘Go and call the elders to come here.’

ref: While the Pharisees were still there, Jesus asked them,
text: Agi Farisianɛatɔ bɛkpɛ likpɔ ɛ, Yesu eʋi ba si,
translated: When the Pharisees heard this, they said, "This is the son of Jesus,"

ref: No one was able to give Jesus an answer, and from that day on, no one dared ask him any more questions.
text: Ɔnɔnɔ ɔ́tani le onu kubu kí Yesu u, lɛ dɔnɔ liwe lɛ klɔ aʋa kɔtsinɔ ɛ, ɔnɔnɔ ɔ́tani yɛ liboeboe kuʋi tɔ ɔ.
translated: The woman who was sick came to Jesus, and when she touched his clothes, the woman became completely well.

ref: If you put yourself above others, you will be put down. But if you humble yourself, you will be honored.
text: Ɔniliɛ gi aabitɛ isu ya ɔnɔ ʋidi i, Aya aaple ni ye lɛ ɔniliɛ gi aaple ni isu ya ɛ, Aya aamu ni ye. Yesu ebu kusɔliɔ baka ba gi benu aŋu mɛ beboebitɛ ba lipɔ ɔ
translated: The woman took the fruit and ate it, and she also gave some to her husband, who was with her, and he ate it. Then the eyes of both of them were opened, and they realized they were naked.

ref: So obey everything they teach you, but don't do as they do. After all, they say one thing and do something else.
text: Lɛ loso e, lilɛ si mlabitɛ kidɔ kakaa gi biasi mlɔ si mlabitɛ ɛ aʋa, tɔ mlezeni liboe kakaa gi biasi mlɔ si mlabitɛ kude e; pɔ̀ mlakikpasi ba kiboebitɛ ɛ, lɛ ese linu si baʋíga ní ba bidɔbasɛbasɛ ʋɛ aʋa a.
translated: And so, the person who does good things will receive good things, but the person who does bad things will receive bad things; if you plant corn, you will harvest corn, and if you plant beans, you will harvest beans.

ref: None of you should be called the leader. The Messiah is your only leader.
text: Lɛ loso mlakíki si baxwa mlɔ si, ‘Ɔnɔgbanɔ’, lɛ ese linu si mlɔ ɔnɔgbanɔ tolee ko onu Kristo ye.
translated: And the word that they use, ‘Ɔnɔgbanɔ’, is the same word they use for Jesus Christ.

ref: You Pharisees and teachers of the Law of Moses are in for trouble! You're nothing but show-offs. You travel over land and sea to win one follower. And when you have done so, you make that person twice as fit for hell as you are.
text: “Litraze gagla ɛki mlɔ ɔsã nɔ badɔbasɛ ba ni Farisianɛ ma! Mlɔ banɔ aŋu mɛ beboebitɛ ba! Mlɛdzi kaale ni kaʋa tɔ mleekyi ɔnɔ, pɔ̀ xe mlemɔ ɔnɔ ɛki Aya ɛ, kɔ mlɛbitɛ yɛ ɔnɔ zimizi gi aasɔ̀ ni kifumoni ye me sani mlɔ tutɔ lɔ ɛ!
translated: Do not act like the Pharisees! Do not do what they do! They say one thing and do another, so do not follow their example, because they do not practice what they preach!

ref: And anyone who swears by the temple also swears by God, who lives there.
text: lɛ xe aka kitamu ni Yerusalem Lisɔligba lɛ ɛ, aka kitamu yɛ ni lɛ ni Aya, gi yɛ yɛ̀ ozekɔ ɛ;
translated: When he was approaching Jerusalem, near the Mount of Olives, he sent two of his disciples;

ref: You are nothing but snakes and the children of snakes! How can you escape going to hell?
text: Mlɔ bagblaga ʋa ni bagblaga bebi ʋa! Kitɛ mlegyo edi si mlaase ní lipɔ bubu le gi liakpɛ mlɔ ní kifumoni ye me na a?
translated: Bring the old mat and the new mat! Which one do you think is better to sleep on, the old one or the new one?

ref: It will be a terrible time for women who are expecting babies or nursing young children.
text: Liaze gagla ɛki bamɛdzɛ ba nì bebi ne ba ní ewe ala klɔ mɛ!
translated: The child is crying because his mother and father have not come home!

ref: But this is just the beginning of troubles.
text: Bɛya elue biaze ŋwisi kuyeʋiyeʋi ní kubipɔ mɛ.
translated: They will come tomorrow to help us with the work.

ref: If you are living in Judea at that time, run to the mountains.
text: “Kɔlɔ ɛ, banama gi bali ní Yudea kesea mɛ ɛ, bese atrɛ ní eto la mɛ.
translated: Long ago, there was a man living in the land of Judea, whose name was Zechariah.

ref: When the Son of Man appears, things will be just as they were when Noah lived.
text: Ɔnɔɛ Yebi ye kubaba ɛ, kwiaze ko ŋwisi nite gi lize ní Noa lipoe le me e.
translated: In those days, people were eating and drinking, until the day Noah entered the ark.

ref: No one knows the day or hour. The angels in heaven don't know, and the Son himself doesn't know. Only the Father knows.
text: “Ɔnɔnɔ ote, liwe le loo ɔnyɔsasa nɔ, Aya yɛ baxwɛdzɛ ba ní lifunu ne loo Ɔnɔɛ Yebi ye kyɛ béte e, Ɔka Aya tete ko ete.
translated: The chief is here, people are gathered at the chief's house, Aya and the elders are sitting with the chief Yebi to discuss, the chief Aya spoke first.

ref: People were eating, drinking, and getting married right up to the day the flood came and Noah went into the big boat.
text: Kpɛ ɔtɔ kí kuni yiyio e, banima bɛzɛŋa, lɛ bɛzɛŋwɛ kyɛ, benyime nì badzɛ bɛzɛgbani baglɔ ʋa, kɔzɛna nani liwe gɔgɔ lɛ aʋa gi Noa emu eku ní onitsahu lo me e.
translated: When the flood came, people were destroyed, but those who listened to the words of God and entered the ark with Noah were saved.

ref: Two men will be in the same field, but only one will be taken. The other will be left.
text: Ní lipoe lɛ klɔ mɛ ɛ, benyime tiaʋa biazɛwa axwɛ ní ɔnyrɔ mɛ, biasɛ ni tole, lɛ ɔʋla ɛ, biayurɔ yɛ ɛ.
translated: When I went there, I saw a woman sitting with her child, and the child was crying.

ref: But suppose one of the servants thinks the master won't return until late.
text: Pɔ̀ xe onu ɔnɔsumu nyani nyani i, aasi isu ya sì, yɛ ɔkaɛ ɔ́tratsɔ odzi i,
translated: If someone does something bad, and you see it, you should tell the truth about it.

ref: Servants are fortunate if their master comes and finds them doing their job.
text: Liaku ɔnɔsumu liɛ klɔ isami xe yɛ ɔkaɛ adziba tsani yɛ gi eedi kepea aʋa mumɔ ɛ!
translated: The children quickly ran to the river so that they could swim before the rain started!

ref: The ones who were wise took along extra oil for their lamps.
text: Pɔ̀ idisami beboete ba ɛ, bevu kumu ní kuʋeo me epɔni ba kani ʋa.
translated: If you do not listen to advice, you will have problems in your life.

ref: So, my disciples, always be ready! You don't know the day or the time when all this will happen. A Story about Three Servants (Luke
text: Lɛ Yesu agù yɛ abee si lɛ loso e, mleze ni aŋu lɛ ese linu si mléte liwe le e loo ɔnyɔsasa nɔ ɔ, gi maadziba ɛ. Yesu ebu abee ni banɔsumu tiata ba su
translated: When Jesus had finished saying these things, the crowds were amazed at his teaching. Jesus called the children to come to him.

ref: Everyone who has something will be given more, and they will have more than enough. But everything will be taken from those who don't have anything.
text: Ɔnɔ kakaa liɛ awla gi kidɔ kilɛ ɛ, biapɔnɔ ɔki yɛ, tɔ aamɔ sanɔ; pɔ̀ ɔniliɛ awla gi kididɔ kíma ɛ, kisisami liɛ gi kiliɛ yɛ awla ɛ, biazɛ kɛ.
translated: The one who eats a little today and keeps some, when hunger comes, he will have something to eat; but the one who eats everything today and keeps nothing, when hunger comes, he will have nothing.

ref: When I was hungry, you gave me something to eat, and when I was thirsty, you gave me something to drink. When I was a stranger, you welcomed me,
text: Gi kuʋɛɔ kwize kume e, mlɛki mɛ kiŋaŋa, olile ezeku me lɛ mlɛki mɛ kuni; Meze ɔbaba lɛ mlɛzi mɛ kpɛ mlɔ kupeo me.
translated: If you want to go there, you must be strong, because the road is not easy; Many people have tried but only a few have succeeded.

ref: The king will answer, “Whenever you did it for any of my people, no matter how unimportant they seemed, you did it for me.”
text: Nite si Okusi ye aabu le onu si, ‘Mɛsi mlɔ anukwarɛɛ si, lipoe kakaa gi mlɛbitɛ nite me eboa la elu ɛki mɛ benemi ba ya mɛ tole e gi ɔnɔnɔ oʋíbù i, mɛ mlɛbitɛ lɛ ɛkɔ.’
translated: Then Okusi said to his father, ‘I will go and work in the farm, but after a while when I got there I changed my mind and did not go.’

ref: I was hungry, but you did not give me anything to eat, and I was thirsty, but you did not give me anything to drink.
text: Kuʋɛ kwiku me pɔ̀ mlɛda aŋu na mláki mɛ kiŋaŋa a, olile eku me mláki mɛ kuni i;
translated: If you do not have money in your pocket, even your relatives will not recognize you.

ref: I was a stranger, but you did not welcome me, and I was naked, but you did not give me any clothes to wear. I was sick and in jail, but you did not take care of me.”
text: Meze ɔbaba pɔ̀ mlazi mɛ akpɛ mlɔ kupeo me e. Meze ni liya mlegbe mlákpɛ kusa mɛ su u. Gi mazɛɛnya ɛ xe meze ligba kyɛ ɛ, mlákɔ litɔtɔ ní le mɛ aki mɛ ɛ.’
translated: If a child does not listen to advice, he will get into trouble. If he listens to advice, he will avoid trouble. That is why elders say, "Advice is good for those who listen to it."

ref: The king will say to them, “Whenever you failed to help any of my people, no matter how unimportant they seemed, you failed to do it for me.”
text: Niteme si Okusi ye aasi ba si, ‘Mɛɛsi mlɔ si, lipoe kakaa ɛ lɛ gi mlegbe xe mlapɔni mɛ benemi ba ya gi ɔnɔnɔ óʋibu i, mɛ mlapɔnɔ.’
translated: Jesus answered him, ‘Truly I say to you, today you will be with me in paradise.’

ref: Finally, Jesus returned to his disciples and said, “Are you still sleeping and resting? The time has come for the Son of Man to be handed over to sinners.
text: Lɛ edzì atrɛ ní badɔkpasi ba klɔ ɛ, xe eʋi ba si “Sra sɛ ko mlafini idɔ xe mlɛyɔ isu ya mɛ na a? Mledi ɔnyɔsasa nɔ ɛna! Bɛtra bu Ɔnɔɛ Yebi ye kpɛ awla ɛki banɔ zimizi ba.
translated: So after the meal was finished, the child asked, “Grandma, why do you always put the soup on top of the fufu before giving it to me? I want to know today! Please tell me the story of how this custom began.”

ref: This is my blood, and with it God makes his agreement with you. It will be poured out, so that many people will have their sins forgiven.
text: “Bɛ ya binu mɛ ika lɛ gi bɛkɔ e du kunyanya vuvu yɔ ní Aya ni yɛ banima kusi, mɛ ika lɛ gi bɛbali ní banɔ glegle ba eboe zimizi na kukɔkyɛ litukpo o.
translated: If you go there, you will see the place where Aya and her friends used to play, and you will also see the big trees and the river flowing beside the old stone bridge.

ref: Jesus went with his disciples to a place called Gethsemane. When they got there, he told them, “Sit here while I go over there and pray.”
text: Lɛ Yesu ni yɛ badɔkpasi ba bɛtrɛ ní ɔkutɔ gi bizɛxwa si Getsemane e, lɛ asi ba si, “Mleze ní ya tɔ mitrɛ ní ɔtɔ nɔ kɔlɔ tɔ mizɛɛxwa Aya.”
translated: And Jesus and his disciples went to a place called Gethsemane, and he said to his disciples, "Sit here while I go over there and pray."

ref: But Peter said, “Even if I have to die with you, I will never say I don't know you.” All the others said the same thing. Jesus Prays (Mark
text: Lɛ Petro ebu le onu si, “Xe liaze si beye me apɔni wɔ kyɛ ɛ, mátranada si mete wo!” Lɛ badɔkpasi gɔgɔ ba kyɛ bɛpani nite ke e. Yesu axwa Aya ní Getsemane
translated: Then Peter answered him, “Even if everyone else abandons you, I will never leave you!” The disciples all said the same thing. Jesus prays at Gethsemane.

ref: One of Jesus' followers pulled out a sword. He struck the servant of the high priest and cut off his ear.
text: Banama gi beze nì Yesu kude e, bamɛ tole etso yɛ ihɛ piɔpiɔ yɛ lɛ ati kusumu yo ɔka ʋidi ye yɛ ɔnɔdzidzi ye yɛ kutukpa ɛ.
translated: The people who believe in Jesus, their hearts are clean and they live in peace and truth and in righteousness and in love.

ref: The high priest then tore his robe and said, “This man claims to be God! We don't need any more witnesses! You have heard what he said.
text: Lɛ kusumu yo Ɔka ʋidi ye akyì yɛ ɔtutɔ yɛ liwu le tɔ yikɔ ɛbasɛ yɛ kusuyeʋio, lɛ apani si, “Libusioboe! kúdzi uzia ɔnɔ gi eze liboe le katsalia tɔɔ. Mlenu yɛ libusioboe le gi apani i.
translated: In the evening when the sun had set and darkness had covered the land, the elders gathered, and one said, “Listen! Tonight something important will happen in the village. Let us all listen to what the elder has to say.”

ref: But Jesus did not answer. So the high priest said, “With the living God looking on, you must tell the truth. Are you the Messiah, the Son of God?”
text: Pɔ̀ Yesu ɔfuɛ, nì yɛ ɛ. Kusumu yo Ɔka ʋidi ye asi Yesu dzɛ si “Maka kitamu akí wɔ ní Aya linyi ne me, si blɔ xe wɔ wonu Kristo ye gi onu Aya Yebi ye e?”
translated: Because Jesus died, we are saved. The darkness could not overcome Jesus so that “If anyone believes in me, even though he dies, he will live, and whoever lives and believes in me will never die?”

ref: After Jesus had been arrested, he was led off to the house of Caiaphas the high priest. The nation's leaders and the teachers of the Law of Moses were meeting there.
text: Banama gi bevu Yesu i, bɛtsini yɛ ní Kayafa gi onu Kusumu yo Ɔka ʋidi ye kepea mɛ, níklɔ ɔsã nɔ badɔbasɛ ba nì banɔtsitsi ba bɛtsa.
translated: They brought Jesus first to Annas, who was the father-in-law of Caiaphas, the high priest that year.

ref: But Peter followed along at a distance and came to the courtyard of the high priest's palace. He went in and sat down with the guards to see what was going to happen.
text: Petro fɛ eze ní ba kedea ke. Agà riɖiɖi eti eku ní kusumu yo ɔkaɛ kepea mɛ. Eze kesea ní kepea mɛ kɔpɔni kepea bepepe ba tɔ yimɔ kiliɛ gi eboe la aatrɛ ɛ.
translated: Petro said that he will go. The child was crying because his mother did not buy him what he wanted. He said that what he wanted was a new shirt and shoes to wear to the festival.

ref: Again Peter denied it, and this time he swore, “I don't even know that man!”
text: Petro anada dzɛ si, “Maka kitamu si méte onyime liɛ klɔ!”
translated: "Peter answered saying, 'I have never eaten anything unclean!'"

ref: While Peter was sitting out in the courtyard, a servant girl came up to him and said, “You were with Jesus from Galilee.”
text: Lɛ gi Petro eze ní kusumu yo ɔka ʋidi ye yɛ kepea kuka mɛ ɛ, ɔdzɛ osumubi ɔtɔ eti apɔni Petro lɛ asi yɛ si, “Wɔfɛ woze ni Yesu Galilea ɔnɛ kude.”
translated: But Peter was sitting outside in the courtyard, and a servant girl came to him and said, “You also were with Jesus of Galilee.”

ref: This is why people still call that place “Field of Blood.”
text: Lɛ loso bizɛxwa kesea lia klɔ si ìka kese kɔkpɛ nì omono o.
translated: And so the chief called all the elders and told them everything.

ref: And when the chief priests and leaders brought their charges against him, he did not say a thing.
text: Pɔ̀ gi kusumu yo baka ba nì banɔtsitsi ba bɛkɔ eboe la ní yɛ su i, óbu litɔtɔ onu u.
translated: If you want to do something good for your friend, do it while they are still alive.

ref: But Jesus did not say anything, and the governor was greatly amazed. The Death Sentence (Mark
text: Pɔ̀ Yesu obu liboeboe onu tɔɔ gi lítu kesea ɔʋadi ye kugyɔ ɔ. Pilato atsa kutsépia akí Yesu
translated: But Jesus did not answer them a single word, so that the governor was greatly amazed. Pilate questioned Jesus again.

ref: But the chief priests and the leaders convinced the crowds to ask for Barabbas to be set free and for Jesus to be killed.
text: Kusumu yo baka ba nì banɔtsitsi ba bɛliɔʋi linɔkpi le tɔ liʋi Pilato tɔ ɔyurɔ Baraba ɔki ba tɔ beye Yesu boŋu.
translated: The people gathered and brought their leaders and took Jesus to Pilate, and they asked Pilate to release Barabbas and to kill Jesus.

ref: Everyone answered, “We and our own families will take the blame for his death!”
text: Lɛ linɔkpi le pɔ́ libu le onu si, “Ki yɛ kutseo kɔ kutuheheo kuba blɔ ni blɔ bebi ba aʋa a!”
translated: And the woman said to the snake, “We may eat the fruit of the trees in the garden!”

ref: There they gave Jesus some wine mixed with a drug to ease the pain. But when Jesus tasted what it was, he refused to drink it.
text: Ni klɔ bɛki Yesu kuda gi bɛtsa ni bidɔ tiitii bitɔ; pɔ̀ gi akpɛ kɔ onu edi i ɔŋwɛ kɔ.
translated: And they took Jesus' body and wrapped it with linen cloths and placed it in a tomb where no one had ever been laid.

ref: Others said, “Wait! Let's see if Elijah will come and save him.”
text: Pɔ̀ banɔ gɔgɔ ba bɛpani si, “Lɛɛ mlaki tɔ kudi sì Eliya atra bu ye na a!”
translated: Some people were saying, “Maybe Elijah has come to take him down!”

ref: At once the curtain in the temple was torn in two from top to bottom. The earth shook, and rocks split apart.
text: Ni lipoe lɛ klɔ mɛ ɛ, Yerusalem Lisɔligba lɛ mɛ kusa gi bɛkɔ ɛtsa lɛ mɛ ɛ, kwikyi idɔnɔ lifune banani kesea. Lɛ kesea kesugu u, xe aklakpɔ la ɛyaʋi i.
translated: When they approached and saw it, Jerusalem the Holy City appeared before them, shining brightly in the morning sun. The sun was rising, and the children were amazed.

ref: Many women had come with Jesus from Galilee to be of help to him, and they were there, looking on at a distance.
text: Badzɛ glegle gi beze nì yɛ kude e dɔni Galilea xe bezee di yɛ baziazia ba aʋa ɛ, bezee di bidɔ ɛdɔni ɔdzage no.
translated: Now after Jesus was born in Bethlehem of Judea in the days of Herod the king, behold, wise men from the East came to Jerusalem.

ref: On the next day, which was a Sabbath, the chief priests and the Pharisees went together to Pilate.
text: Keletitia gi keze Lizemɔniwe le e, kusumu yo baka ba ni Farisianɛ ma bɛzɛtsani Pilato,
translated: Early in the morning on the Sabbath, the chief priests and the Pharisees went to Pilate,

ref: They said, “Sir, we remember what this liar said while he was still alive. He claimed in three days he would come back from death.
text: lɛ bɛsi yɛ si, “Ɔka, kwiplɛ aŋu si lipoɛ lɛ gi ɔsia nɛ liɛ klɔ ezè ligbã́ nɛ ɛ, apani aplɛ si, ‘Mayɔ ní ewe tata kede’
translated: When they arrived there, he said, "Alright, let us sit here and rest for a while," and then he added, 'Let us not hurry.'

ref: Jesus' eleven disciples went to a mountain in Galilee, where Jesus had told them to meet him.
text: Lɛ yɛ badɔkpasi liɔfɔ ni tole ba ɛ, bɛtrɛ ní ketokpaa aʋa ní Galilea ní niklɔ gi Yesu asi ba si batrɛ ɛ.
translated: And on the third day there was a wedding in Cana of Galilee, and the mother of Jesus was there.

ref: Then Jesus said, “Don't be afraid! Tell my followers to go to Galilee. They will see me there.” Report of the Guard
text: Lɛ Yesu asi ba si, “Mlakíse lifu, mlazɛ si mɛ benemi ba si batrɛ ní Galilea, lɛ niklɔ biamɔ mɛ ɛ.” Banɔpe ba kunutsatsaa
translated: When Jesus arose, he said, “Do not be afraid, go and tell my brothers to go to Galilee, there they will see me.” The women hurried away.

ref: John wore clothes made of camel's hair. He had a leather strap around his waist and ate grasshoppers and wild honey.
text: Yohanes yɛ bidɔkpɛkpɛ ʋɛ bize kposɔ sipi liwu; anya yɛ kasɛa mɛ ni kagaplekpa ɔgɛmi, lɛ yɛ bidɔŋaŋa ʋɛ bize akikli la nì liŋwafu mɛ kibú.
translated: John was a lamp that burned and gave light; you were willing for a time to rejoice in his light, but I have a greater testimony than John's.

ref: Jesus told the evil spirit, “Be quiet and come out of the man!”
text: Yesu akpɛ ozi ní ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ aʋa si, “Gɔ tsriɖi! Do báza ní ɔnɔɛ mɛ!”
translated: Jesus saw the crowd and said, “Take heart! Come to me!”

ref: The spirit shook him. Then it gave a loud shout and left.
text: Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ esugu onyimee gaglaa, ɛkpɛ ozi tatata, lɛ edo eza ní ɔnɔɛ mɛ ɛ.
translated: The butterfly flies gently in the morning, searching everywhere, as if looking for something important.

ref: Everyone was completely surprised and kept saying to each other, “What is this? It must be some new kind of powerful teaching! Even the evil spirits obey him.”
text: Banima pɔ́ ba inugu itse, lɛ bekpese ba ahanuglɔ ba kuʋi si, “Liboe woli lɛ ya a? Bidɔbasɛbasɛ vuvu woli lu bɛ ya a? Onyime liɛ kɔ kigagla kilɛ yɛ awla gi izɛɛki ifuɛfuɛ zimizi ɔsã a, lɛ izeenu kutu yɔ fɛ ɛ.”
translated: The elders sat under the tree, and the children gathered around them and asked, “What is this story? Where did the olden days begin? Tell us so we may know how our ancestors lived, and so we too may learn from it.”

ref: Jesus felt sorry for the man. So he put his hand on him and said, “I want to! Now you are well.”
text: Asigaga la ɛga Yesu nì onyimee; anina yɛ ɔwla lɔ, lɛ agbɔni onyimee e. Lɛ Yesu asi yɛ si, “Linu mɛ kupɛpɛ; wɔ isu ya idra a!”
translated: When Jesus got out of the boat, a man with an unclean spirit met him. He lived among the tombs, and no one could bind him anymore. When Jesus saw him, he said, "Come out of this man, you evil spirit!"

ref: When they got there, Jesus was told that Simon's mother-in-law was sick in bed with fever.
text: Simon yɛnadzee, kuwe kwivu ye plɛ ɔtɔsi aʋa, lɛ gi Yesu eku kepea mɛ ko e, bɛki yɛ kutète kyɔŋ.
translated: Simon was amazed, because many fish were caught, so he knelt before Jesus and said, "Lord, leave me, for I am a sinful man."

ref: It was during the time of Abiathar the high priest. David went into the house of God and ate the sacred loaves of bread that only priests are allowed to eat. He also gave some to his followers.”
text: Nite si atrɛ Aya kepea mɛ gi azɛɛta liklã dradra lɛ gi bɛbla plɛ ki Aya ɛ. Liba lɛ mɛ nite lipoe lɛ klɔ gi Abiata eze Kusumu yo Ɔka ʋidi ye e. Ni ɔsã nɔ onu i, kusumu yo baka ba ko biata liklã ɛnɛ klɔ xe e, pɔ̀ Dawid atà nɛ lɛ aki yɛ banima kyɛ ɛ.”
translated: When Aya was going to the farm, she met a woman who told her that Aya should not go. The woman said that if she went, she would meet Abiata at Kusumu and trouble would happen there. If she did not listen, trouble would come and Abiata would fight with her, just as David fought with the lion.

ref: Jesus finished by saying, “People were not made for the good of the Sabbath. The Sabbath was made for the good of people.
text: Lɛ Yesu agù yɛ liboe le si, “Lizemɔniwe le ɛ, bɛbitɛ lɛ ɛki banɔ kupɛ̀pɛ, bábitɛ banima aki Lizemɔniwe le e.
translated: But Jesus said to them, “Let the children come to me, do not stop them, for the kingdom of God belongs to such as these.”

ref: But the time will come when he will be taken from them. Then they will go without eating.
text: Pɔ̀ liwe etɔ litraba gi biagbani ɔdzɛnyee sɛnɔ ní basu u. Lipoe lɛ klɔ bianya onugu lo.
translated: He bought three books and gave them to his younger sibling. The child was very happy about it.

ref: Jesus told the man to stand up where everyone could see him.
text: Yesu asi onyimee si, “Ga lɛɛ ní ɔtɔ nɔ ya ní banima aŋu na mɛ.”
translated: Jesus said to him, “Foxes have holes and birds of the air have nests, but the Son of Man has nowhere to lay his head.”

ref: Jesus decided to ask some of his disciples to go up on a mountain with him, and they went.
text: Gi Yesu asɛ ní klɔ ɛ, emu ketokpa katɔ lɛ axwa banama gi apɛ ɛ, lɛ bɛba yɛ klɔ ɛ.
translated: When Jesus was born, wise men from the east came to him, and they worshipped him.

ref: So if Satan fights against himself, that will be the end of him.
text: Lɛ loso e, xegi Abosam yɛ kaʋaŋaa kɛlɛ kluʋikluʋi i, katrátani kuze lipoe gblamadza, kiayaʋi kɔ kedu i.
translated: So then, the gods took a small stone, broke it into pieces, and scattered them on the ground.

ref: Jesus asked, “Who is my mother and who are my brothers?”
text: Yesu ebu le onu si, “Nyaŋwɛ onu mone e? Lɛ nyaŋwɛ ba benu mɛ benemi ba a?”
translated: Jesus turned and asked, “What do you want? What do you want me to do for you?”

ref: Then he looked at the people sitting around him and said, “Here are my mother and my brothers.
text: Lɛ edi banama gi bedi iklani yɛ ɛ, lɛ apani si, “Mledi! mone liɛ ya, lɛ mɛ benemi ba ba ya a!
translated: When they finished eating and were about to leave, the woman said, “Wait! Listen to me, I have something to tell you!”

ref: Now listen! A farmer went out to scatter seed in a field.
text: “Mlata kutu! Onyime ɔtɔ eze gi atrɛ amu na oglu kɔ ɔ.
translated: Close the door! My father said you should come and see him in his room.

ref: Some other seeds fell where thornbushes grew up and choked out the plants. So they did not produce any grain.
text: Amubiatɔ fɛ ɛwɔli eku ní bibu ʋe me. Gi bibu ʋe bitsì i, biwà ɛda ní amubi yɛyɛ la aʋa, niteme si amu na ébu libii tɔtɔ ɔ.
translated: Children like to play under this tree. When they are playing, they forget to go home, because the shade of the tree is very cool.

ref: Then when harvest season comes and the grain is ripe, the farmer cuts it with a sickle. A Mustard Seed (Matthew
text: Xegi amu na ɛzã ɛ, kɔ ɔnyrɔmɛdzɛɛ ekpese la kupa ɛ. Lɛ ese linu si la lipa nɛ le lina a.” Abee ní lisebi sisami nɛ su u
translated: "If you want to eat that, go and ask the woman over there. She is the one who cooked it." That is what my mother told me.

ref: Suddenly a storm struck the lake. Waves started splashing into the boat, and it was about to sink.
text: Lɛ lisɛ nɛ ɛ, liwɔ gagla ɛtɔ likpese kufu niteme si kuni yo akpɔ la ɛgbigba eku katapokpaa mɛ gi keyi pɔ́ ɔ.
translated: And then, they took a big stone and placed it at the entrance so that no one could enter the house again.

ref: Jesus was in the back of the boat with his head on a pillow, and he was asleep. His disciples woke him and said, “Teacher, don't you care that we're about to drown?”
text: Yesu afɛ ní katapokpaa mɛ kedea ɔza lɔ gi adɔ sra ní sumu ye aʋa ɛ. Yɛ badɔkpasi ba benyime ye lɛ beʋi ye si, “Ɔdɔbasɛɛ, wɔ́kɔ lɛ mɛ ɔki blɔ gi kuhatse na a?”
translated: "Jesus sat down opposite the offering box and watched the people putting money into it. Many rich people put in large amounts. Then a poor widow came and put in two very small copper coins, worth only a few cents."

ref: Jesus got up and ordered the wind and the waves to be quiet. The wind stopped, and everything was calm.
text: Yesu ayɔ ɔlɛɛ kedu ya. Lɛ akpɛ ozi lo ní liwɔ lɛ aʋa si, “Gɔ tsriɖi!” Lɛ ekyi eku kuni yo akpɔ la aʋa lɛ apani si, “Dɔ zĩi!” Liwɔ lɛ onu liyɔ kyɔŋ lɛ niklɔ petee ɛgɔ kranaa a.
translated: Jesus saw the fig tree. He went closer to see if he would find anything on it, but when he got to the tree, he found only leaves. The tree had no fruit because it was not the season for figs.

ref: She had gone to many doctors, and they had not done anything except cause her a lot of pain. She had paid them all the money she had. But instead of getting better, she only got worse.
text: Bawabitɛ glegle bekeni idi pɔ̀ liboeboe lísɛ ɛ. Ezizi yɛ exo la petee tɔ nya yɛ isu ya mɛ idzi iku lɛ kiku ye, pɔ̀ lɛ́ kinyani boŋ lizɛɛnyani itrɔ ɛ.
translated: The children ran quickly to the riverbank. They were happy to see the water and played there for a long time, enjoying the coolness and the sound of the flowing river.

ref: When they came to Jesus, they saw the man who had once been full of demons. He was sitting there with his clothes on and in his right mind, and they were terrified.
text: niteme si gi bɛba Yesu klɔ ɛ, bɛmɔ onyime liɛ klɔ mɛ gi ifuɛfuɛ zimizi likpi le lize bázanɔ ɛ. Bemɔ yɛ gi odi i, akpɛ awu la mɔmɔmɔ gi eze ní sínɔ mɛ, nite si lifu ne liku banima pɔ́.
translated: When Jesus was born, wise men from the east came and saw a bright star in the sky. They followed it and found the place where the child was, and they were filled with great joy.

ref: Once again Jesus got into the boat and crossed Lake Galilee. Then as he stood on the shore, a large crowd gathered around him.
text: Yesu edzì atsà limu ne atrɛ ní oge lɔ kɔlɔ aʋa. Gi eku níklɔ ɛ, linɔkpi ʋidi itɔ lixogo ní yɛ su.
translated: Jesus took five loaves and two fish. After giving thanks, he broke them and gave them to the people.

ref: The person in charge of the synagogue was also there. His name was Jairus, and when he saw Jesus, he went over to him. He knelt at Jesus' feet
text: Lɛ Yairo gi eze Yudanɛ ma ɔnɔtsitsi ní ba ɔtsaklɔ ɛ, yɛ fɛ aba eku i. Gi amɔ Yesu i, adɔ ní Yesu ketu ya
translated: When Herod was king of Judea, there was a man named Zechariah. He had a wife named Elizabeth. They were the parents of John, who was called John the Baptist.

ref: At that moment Jesus felt power go out from him. He turned to the crowd and asked, “Who touched my clothes?”
text: Lɛ onu klɔ ko e, Yesu amɔ egyesi sì kigagla kido ní yɛ mɛ. Ekyi ní ɔnɔha lɔ gi eze ní yɛ su kusi yo lɛ eʋi si, “Nyaŋwɛ agbɔni mɛ awu la ɛ?”
translated: When they arrived there, Jesus saw a large crowd and felt compassion for them. He asked his disciples, “Where can we buy bread for these people to eat?”

ref: But Jesus said, “Prophets are honored by everyone, except the people of their hometown and their relatives and their own family.”
text: Nite si Yesu asi ba si, “Bizɛkpɛ kubùbù ní Aya yɛ beboedoplɛ ba su ní ɔkɔ kakaa. Pɔ̀ ní ba kepea ɛ, ba akɔanɛ ma nì ba benete ba kusi kɔ ɛ, beboedoplɛ ba béyi kididɔ ní ba aŋu mɛ ɛ.”
translated: Then Jesus said to them, “Truly, truly I say to you, unless a grain of wheat falls into the earth and dies, it remains alone; but if it dies, it bears much fruit.”

ref: because Herod was afraid of John and protected him. He knew that John was a good and holy man. Even though Herod was confused by what John said, he was glad to listen to him. And he often did.
text: Lɛ Okusi ye Herodes pɔ ɛ, azeese Yohanes, lɛ ese linu si etè si Yohanes eze ɔnɔ gbã, lɛ yɛ isu fɛ idra ní Aya Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ mɛ. Lɛ loso e, edi le aʋa si kididɔ kísa Yohanes kura ní ligba lɛ ɛ. Yohanes yɛ eboe pani pani la ezetu Okusi ye Herodes lipoe kakaa gi enu la ɛ; pɔ̀ fɛ azɛɛpɛ si yinu la.
translated: When King Herod heard about this, he said that John, whom he beheaded, had been raised from the dead. For Herod himself had given orders to have John arrested, and he had him bound and put in prison. He did this because of Herodias, his brother Philip’s wife, whom he had married. For John had been saying to Herod, “It is not lawful for you to have your brother’s wife.”

ref: I swear that I will give you as much as half of my kingdom, if you want it.”
text: Niteme si aka bitamu glegle aki ɔnuvɔɛ, lɛ atrɛ lɛ aʋa si, “Matàni kidɔ kakaa si, kidɔ kakaa gi wɔaʋi i, vaa xegi mɛ kaʋaŋaa kapɛ tiele fɛ ɛ, maaki wɔ kɛ.”
translated: The woman took the small basket and said, "Children, come here, come here quickly, bring your hands so I can put the food in them, and then you can go."

ref: The girl left and asked her mother, “What do you think I should ask for?” Her mother answered, “The head of John the Baptist!”
text: Niteme si, ɔdzɛʋi yɛ edo atrɛ ní yene klɔ lɛ azeeʋi ye si, “Mone, ege maaʋi i?” Niklɔ yene asi yɛ si, “Ʋi Yohanes Kuni yo Ɔkpɛkpɛ yɛ litukpo le!”
translated: That night, a child went to his father and asked, “Father, where is my mother?” The father replied, “John the Baptist is in the wilderness!”

ref: Herod was very sorry for what he had said. But he did not want to break the promise he had made in front of his guests.
text: Liboe le likɔ si Okusi ye Herodes awɔ ni asigaga mɛ. Pɔ̀ yɛ bitamu ʋɛ gi aka ní yɛ banɔxwaxwa ba aŋu mɛ loso e, ɔ́pɛ si yigbe ɛki yɛ ɛ.
translated: After Jesus was born in Bethlehem in Judea, during the time of King Herod, wise men from the east came to Jerusalem and asked, "Where is the one who has been born king of the Jews?"

ref: That evening the disciples came to Jesus and said, “This place is like a desert, and it's already late.
text: Lipoe lɛ gi kelea kekpese kublo e, yɛ badɔkpasi ba bɛba lɛ bɛsi yɛ si, “Niya ebu lidze” lɛ “Kelea keeblo nì blo.
translated: The elders sat together to discuss, and after a while one of them said, "If this is true" or "Let us speak the truth."

ref: Jesus replied, “You give them something to eat.” But they asked him, “Don't you know it would take almost a year's wages to buy all of these people something to eat?”
text: Lɛ Yesu asi ba si, “Mlɔ tutɔ mlaki ba bidɔ tɔ baŋa.” Lɛ bɛsi yɛ si, “Kibɔɛ liɛ gi kwiazizi ní ba aklã na litukpo e, kiana ɔnɔ tole yɛ badzidzi ɔgɔtiaʋa liʋɛ ɛ!”
translated: When Jesus came, he said, “Let the children come to me.” Then they said, “We have never seen anything like this before, how he teaches with such authority!”

ref: Jesus' disciples picked up twelve large baskets of leftover bread and fish.
text: Pɔ̀ gi bɛŋa pɔ ɛ, badɔkpasi ba bɛkpɛ bidɔ mɛ gi bɛŋa ɛgɔ likpɔ, gi biyi kusɔʋi liɔfɔ ni tuʋa a.
translated: If someone is walking and meets a snake on the road, they should be careful and not step on it.

ref: He could see that the disciples were struggling hard, because they were rowing against the wind. Not long before morning, Jesus came toward them. He was walking on the water and was about to pass the boat.
text: Amɔ si yɛ badɔkpasi ba bɛzetutru ni katapokpaa ní limu ne aʋa lɛ ese linu si bɛtsani liwɔ lɛ gi lifu idɔni niklɔ gi bɛzɛtrɛ ɛ. Lɛ loso olezatɔ nɔ ŋwisi ɔkukɔ tɔpu nɔ kimemee me kinɛ ɛ, Yesu aga kuni yo aʋa aba tsani ba. Nya apɛ si yiza ba aʋa xe e.
translated: The person who is truly pure in heart and mind, who does not do evil things or speak lies, will live in peace with God. Such a person will receive blessings from the Lord, and Jesus says that they will see God. This is the good news for those who follow Him.

ref: As soon as they got out of the boat, the people recognized Jesus.
text: Agi beple ní katapokpaa mɛ pɔ́ ko e, banima bɛmɔ Yesu igyesi kyɔŋ.
translated: When they arrived at the grave, they saw that Jesus was not there.

ref: You are good at rejecting God's commands so that you can follow your own teachings!
text: Lɛ Yesu atrɛ yɛ liboe le aʋa si, “Ni mlɔ kuboeteo me e, mlɛyurɔ Aya isã nɛ ɛplɛ lɛ mlevu isu i kɔ ni mlɔ tutɔ mlɔ isã nɛ ɛ.
translated: And Jesus said to them, "When you pray, say, 'Our Father who is in heaven, hallowed be your name. Let your kingdom come. Give us each day our daily bread.'"

ref: The Pharisees and many others obey the teachings of their ancestors. They always wash their hands in the proper way before eating.
text: Farisianɛ ma ni Yudanɛ ma pɔ́, bekuni ba bakatsi ba bidɔbasɛbasɛ bɛ kude, nite si xegi béplo awla la ní ligoʋi le onu i, bátraŋa bidɔ ɔ.
translated: The Pharisees and the Jews gathered, and the elders and the teachers of the law met, and they brought Jesus before them to question him.

ref: Jesus replied: You are nothing but show-offs! The prophet Isaiah was right when he wrote that God had said, “All of you praise me with your words, but you never really think about me.
text: Lɛ Yesu ebu lɛ onu si, “Yesaya liboe le ligyɔ koŋ gi amɔ liboe edo aplɛ ní mlɔ su i! Mlɔ banɔ aŋu mɛ beboebitɛ mlenu, kiliɛ gi aŋwlimi aplɛ si i: Aya si, “ ‘Banɔ ba ya ɛ, enugumeboe ko bɛkɔ ɛlɔ mɛ ɛ; pɔ̀ ba ina nɛ kɔ ívu me kura.
translated: And Jesus said to them, “Isaiah was right when he prophesied about you hypocrites, as it is written: ‘These people honor me with their lips, but their hearts are far from me. They worship me in vain; their teachings are merely human rules.’”

ref: You won't let those people help their parents.
text: kɔ libasɛ si mlɛki yɛ kudeo si ɔkipɔni yɛka loo yene e.
translated: Take the book that is on the table and give it to the child who is sitting there.

ref: Then Jesus said: What comes from your heart is what makes you unclean.
text: Lɛ atrɛ ní yɛ liboe le aʋa si, “Kidiliɛ gi kido edɔní ɔnɔse lo me e, kɛ kizelulu ɔnɔ ɔ.
translated: And then the woman said to her child, “Do not go outside today, stay here with me.”

ref: After Jesus and his disciples had left the crowd and gone into the house, they asked him what these sayings meant.
text: Gi asɛ ní linɔkpi le su xe atrɛ ní kepea mɛ ɛ, yɛ badɔkpasi ba beʋi ye si obu abee liɛ ya ese ɔki ba.
translated: If a child does not listen to advice and continues to be stubborn, the elders say that such a child will learn the hard way.

ref: All of these come from your heart, and they are what make you unfit to worship God. A Woman's Faith (Matthew
text: Eboe dovo la ya petee ɛdɔ ní ɔnɔɛ kimɛ ɛmɛ lɛ la izelulu ɔnɔ ɔ.” Ɔdzɛ ɔtɔ yɛ kuziŋaa ní Yesu me
translated: "Eboe said that he does not know the person who healed his eyes.” This was the response he gave to those who asked him about Jesus.
